  ],
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Section opens with an explicit gap statement (prior methods lack confidence signals) and a contribution claim (temperature-scaled deep ensemble for calibrated uncertainty). Reviewer must verify the gap is real and the framing accurately represents the model's mechanism.",
    "R1.2": "The logical chain from modest AUROC gains (~1.5 pp on ClinVar-2024) to the conclusion 'substantial step forward' requires scrutiny. The high-confidence regime claim (92.4 % vs 84.1 %) is introduced as separate evidence but is not formally tied back to the ECE improvement argument.",
    "R1.3": "Load-bearing: the section asserts specific benchmark figures (AUROC, ECE, high-confidence accuracy) and draws downstream conclusions from them. Any mismatch between the stated numbers, the benchmarks they come from, and the rhetorical conclusions (e.g., 'establishes CalibVar as a substantial step forward') directly undermines the delegation.",
    "R1.4": "The phrase 'We are unaware of any prior method that achieves comparable calibration on the ClinVar-2024 split at the same parameter budget' is a scoped but strong novelty claim. Reviewer must assess whether the qualifier ('same parameter budget') is well-defined and whether known calibrated variant-effect predictors (e.g., EVE, AlphaMissense uncertainty variants) are adequately distinguished.",
    "R1.7": "The only citation is self-referential and anonymized ('Anonymous Authors et al., 2023'), signaling a double-blind submission. The claim of being unaware of comparable prior work constitutes an implicit completeness-of-literature assertion that requires scholarship scrutiny."
  },
  "clarification_needed": false,
  "clarification_question": ""

rationale.R1.1: Section opens with an explicit gap statement (prior methods lack confidence signals) and a contribution claim (temperature-scaled deep ensemble for calibrated uncertainty). Reviewer must verify the gap is real and the framing accurately represents the model's mechanism.
rationale.R1.2: The logical chain from modest AUROC gains (~1.5 pp on ClinVar-2024) to the conclusion 'substantial step forward' requires scrutiny. The high-confidence regime claim (92.4 % vs 84.1 %) is introduced as separate evidence but is not formally tied back to the ECE improvement argument.
rationale.R1.3: Load-bearing: the section asserts specific benchmark figures (AUROC, ECE, high-confidence accuracy) and draws downstream conclusions from them. Any mismatch between the stated numbers, the benchmarks they come from, and the rhetorical conclusions (e.g., 'establishes CalibVar as a substantial step forward') directly undermines the delegation.
rationale.R1.4: The phrase 'We are unaware of any prior method that achieves comparable calibration on the ClinVar-2024 split at the same parameter budget' is a scoped but strong novelty claim. Reviewer must assess whether the qualifier ('same parameter budget') is well-defined and whether known calibrated variant-effect predictors (e.g., EVE, AlphaMissense uncertainty variants) are adequately distinguished.
rationale.R1.7: The only citation is self-referential and anonymized ('Anonymous Authors et al., 2023'), signaling a double-blind submission. The claim of being unaware of comparable prior work constitutes an implicit completeness-of-literature assertion that requires scholarship scrutiny.
